skill.name: release-skills
skill.description: Guides a repository release workflow for baoyu-skills by reviewing changes, updating bilingual changelogs, bumping marketplace version metadata, committing release files, and creating a local version tag.
skill.summary: Prepare baoyu-skills releases with changelog, version, commit, and local tag steps.
skill.author: jimliu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell-command and weak-crypto patterns. Review confirmed the shell findings are intentional git release workflow instructions, while the weak-cryptography findings are false positives from markdown text and examples. The skill is not malicious, but it can modify repository files, create commits, and create tags, so users should review dry-run output before execution.

medium_findings.title: Repository-Modifying Git Release Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to inspect git history, update release files, stage changes, create a release commit, and create a version tag. These operations match the release-management purpose, but they can alter repository state if run without user review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant commands are explicit git release commands in the documented workflow. They are legitimate for this skill, but they modify local repository state.

medium_findings.title: Manual Push Reminder After Local Release
medium_findings.description: The skill reminds the user to push the main branch and version tag after a local release. It explicitly says not to push automatically, which reduces risk, but publishing remains a sensitive release action.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions separate local release creation from remote publishing. The risk is operational rather than malicious because push commands are presented as a post-release user action.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations are markdown prose, headings, table labels, changelog examples, or references to README files. No cryptographic implementation, hashing call, or weak algorithm usage was found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain documentation text and release examples, not cryptographic code. There is only one markdown file in the skill, and no executable crypto API is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in Git Analysis Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow uses command substitution to assign the latest git tag before generating logs and diffs. This is a normal release step, but it depends on the local git repository state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is plainly present and used for git analysis. It is not evidence of malware, but it is still shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Git Commit and Tag Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow stages release files, creates a release commit, and creates a version tag. These commands are expected for release automation but should be reviewed before running.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact git commands are shown in the workflow. The commands are purposeful and local, but they change repository history.

content.value_statement: Manual release steps are easy to miss when changelogs, versions, commits, and tags must stay aligned. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a repeatable release checklist for baoyu-skills.
actual_capabilities: Reviews git changes since the latest version tag.
Classifies release changes into features, fixes, documentation, and maintenance groups.
Chooses patch, minor, or major version guidance from detected changes and user flags.
Updates English and Chinese changelog entries with matching structure.
Updates marketplace version metadata and release-related README files when needed.
Creates a local release commit and version tag, while leaving remote push to the user.
limitations: Designed for the baoyu-skills repository layout and file names.
Requires a git repository with version tags for best change detection.
Does not automatically publish to the remote repository.
Pre-release handling is marked as reserved and may need manual work.

use_cases.title: Maintainer Release Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare a consistent release after merging fixes, features, or documentation updates.

use_cases.title: Reviewer Dry Run
use_cases.description: Preview the proposed version bump, changelog entries, and files before any repository changes are made.

use_cases.title: Bilingual Documentation Sync
use_cases.description: Keep English and Chinese changelogs and README updates aligned during a release.

prompt_templates.title: Preview Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Run release-skills in dry-run mode. Show the latest tag, proposed version, changelog preview, and files that would change.

prompt_templates.title: Create Patch Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a patch release with release-skills. Review changes since the last tag, update changelogs, bump the marketplace version, commit, and tag locally.

prompt_templates.title: Force Minor Release
prompt_templates.scenario: Feature release with explicit version choice
prompt_templates.prompt: Use release-skills with the minor version option. Update README files if new skills or important usage changes are present.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Breaking Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Use release-skills to inspect the pending changes for breaking changes. Recommend whether a major version is appropriate before creating any release commit.

output_examples.input: Dry-run request after several merged changes.
output_examples.output: A release preview listing the latest tag, proposed next version, grouped changes, changelog draft, README update status, and files that would be modified.

output_examples.input: Patch release request for documentation and bug fixes.
output_examples.output: Updated English and Chinese changelogs, bumped marketplace metadata, a local release commit, and a local version tag.

output_examples.input: Minor release request after adding a new skill.
output_examples.output: A minor version recommendation with README synchronization, feature-focused changelog entries, version metadata update, release commit, and local tag.

best_practices: Run dry-run mode first when release changes are complex or mixed.
Review bilingual changelog entries before creating the release commit.
Push the branch and tag manually only after verifying the local release state.
anti_patterns: Using the skill outside the baoyu-skills repository without checking file paths.
Skipping changelog or README review before tagging a release.
Publishing remote branches or tags before validating the local release commit.

faq.question: Does this skill push releases to the remote repository?
faq.answer: No. It creates the local release commit and tag, then reminds the user how to push manually.

faq.question: Can it preview changes before editing files?
faq.answer: Yes. Use dry-run mode to see the proposed version, changelog entries, and affected files.

faq.question: Which files does it manage during release?
faq.answer: It targets changelog files, README files when needed, and marketplace version metadata.

faq.question: How does it choose the next version?
faq.answer: It uses detected change types and optional user flags for patch, minor, or major releases.

faq.question: Does it support Chinese release notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to update English and Chinese changelogs with matching structure.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for any repository?
faq.answer: No. It is tailored to baoyu-skills paths and should be reviewed before use elsewhere.
